nodes.body: Most docs in this repo start with an SPDX/Copyright HTML comment header (e.g., docs/node-requirements.md, docs/protocol.md). This new doc is missing that header; please add it for consistency and licensing clarity.

HW-0102 AC#1 sets the regulator minimum input voltage to 3.5V, but HW-0103 targets a single-cell LiPo supply, which can be below 3.5V for a significant portion of its discharge curve. Either lower the minimum input requirement (e.g., to cover the intended LiPo range) or explicitly require a boost/charger/power-path that guarantees ΓëÑ3.5V to the regulator.

HW-0103 requires battery voltage to be readable via ADC, and HW-0400 requires Γëñ20 ┬╡A deep-sleep current. As written, itΓÇÖs easy to satisfy the ADC requirement with a divider that violates the sleep-current requirement; consider adding an explicit acceptance criterion that the battery sense path is high-impedance and/or switchable so it meets HW-0400.
```suggestion
3. USB and battery inputs safely coexist (USB preferred when present).
4. Battery sense path (divider, buffer, or switch) MUST be high-impedance and/or switchable in deep sleep so that total deep-sleep current, including sensing leakage, complies with HW-0400 (Γëñ 20 ┬╡A).
```

HW-0200 is a Must requirement, but AC#3 says ΓÇ£Second Qwiic connector ΓÇª (Should priority)ΓÇ¥, which mixes priorities inside the acceptance criteria and makes it unclear what is required to claim HW-0200 is met. Consider splitting the second connector into its own HW-02xx requirement (Priority: Should) or rephrasing AC#3 as an optional recommendation rather than part of the pass/fail criteria.

The sensor footprint table uses a nonstandard Markdown table syntax with double leading pipes (`|| ...`). Elsewhere in docs, tables use the standard `| col | ... |` format (e.g., docs/node-requirements.md). Using the standard format will render correctly across GitHub and other Markdown renderers.

The requirement index table in Appendix A also uses double leading pipes (`|| ...`), which is inconsistent with standard Markdown tables used elsewhere in `docs/`. Switching to the standard single-pipe table syntax will avoid rendering issues.

Typo: ΓÇ£boot strappingΓÇ¥ should be ΓÇ£bootstrappingΓÇ¥.
```suggestion
1. All GPIO pins not consumed by I2C, SPI, USB, or bootstrapping
```

HW-0700 requires the board configuration MUST be YAML, but HW-0600 earlier allows ΓÇ£YAML or TOMLΓÇ¥. Please align these requirements (either YAML-only everywhere, or expand HW-0700/schema to support both).
```suggestion
The board configuration MUST be expressed as a YAML or TOML file with a
```

HW-0102 requires an input range starting at 3.5V, but HW-0103 specifies a single-cell LiPo supply (which typically drops below 3.5V near end-of-discharge). Consider lowering the minimum input voltage requirement (or explicitly defining a battery cutoff/boost strategy) so the regulator requirement matches the stated battery source.
```suggestion
1. Input voltage range: 3.0VΓÇô6V to support single-cell LiPo discharge (or a higher minimum with an explicitly defined battery cutoff/boost strategy).
```

The PR description mentions ΓÇ£18 requirementsΓÇ¥, but this document currently defines a larger set (HW-0100 through HW-1003 in the index). Please update the PR description or adjust the spec so they match.

This new doc doesnΓÇÖt include the standard docs header (HTML comment SPDX/Copyright block and the Document status/scope/audience/related block) that other docs in `docs/` use. Please add the same header style for consistency and licensing clarity.

In the pipeline diagram, the schematic output is labeled ΓÇ£.schΓÇ¥, but elsewhere (and in HW-0800) the intended KiCad 8 extension is ΓÇ£.kicad_schΓÇ¥. Please make the diagram consistent with the rest of the spec.
```suggestion
                Γöé  Schematic (.kicad_sch)   Γöé HW-0800
```

This new doc doesnΓÇÖt include the standard docs header (HTML comment SPDX/Copyright block and the Document status/scope/audience/related block) that other docs in `docs/` use. Please add the same header style for consistency and licensing clarity.

HW-0600 says the configuration can be ΓÇ£YAML or TOMLΓÇ¥, but later requirements (HW-0700) mandate YAML-only. Please reconcile the format requirement so the spec is internally consistent.
```suggestion
1. A configuration file (YAML) specifies:
```

The sensor-footprints table is written with leading double pipes (`|| ...`) which renders as an extra empty column in standard Markdown. Other docs in this repo use normal tables with single `|` delimiters; please update this (and the other `||` tables in this document) to standard `| Col | ... |` formatting.

This bullet is self-referential/contradictory: it recommends a second Qwiic connector and says to "see HW-0200 as a Should-priority enhancement", but HW-0200 is already a Must requirement. Consider either removing the parenthetical reference or splitting the second-connector recommendation into its own requirement ID with Should priority.
```suggestion
3. *Recommendation:* provide a second Qwiic connector for daisy-chaining;
   this is encouraged but not required for HW-0200 compliance.
```

The allowed `board.fab` values list `OSHPark`, but elsewhere the design doc/file naming uses `oshpark` (e.g., `oshpark.kicad_dru`). Please pick one canonical spelling/casing for the fab option (and keep it consistent with the rules filename) so the schema/docs donΓÇÖt diverge.
```suggestion
   - `board.fab`: JLCPCB | PCBWay | oshpark | custom DRC rules
```

The net-naming table uses leading double pipes (`|| ...`), which creates an empty first column in standard Markdown rendering. Please convert this (and the other `||` tables in this document) to standard `| Col | ... |` formatting for consistency with the rest of `docs/`.

The determinism section says timestamps are set to the config fileΓÇÖs modification time. File mtimes vary across clones/CI checkouts, so this would prevent bit-identical outputs for the same config + tool version. Prefer a fixed epoch or derive timestamps from the config hash (or omit them entirely) to preserve reproducibility.
```suggestion
2. **Deterministic timestamps**: KiCad file timestamps are either
   omitted or set to a value derived from the config hash or a fixed
   epoch; filesystem modification times are never used.
```

The CLI interface section doesnΓÇÖt list `check-firmware` or an `--allow <check-id>` mechanism, but later sections describe both. Please either add these commands/options to the CLI list (with brief descriptions) or mark them explicitly as future/optional features to avoid conflicting documentation.
```suggestion
sonde-hw validate <config.yaml>                     # Schema check only
sonde-hw validate <config.yaml> --allow <check-id>  # Schema + rules, allowing specific checks
sonde-hw build <config.yaml>                        # Full pipeline
sonde-hw build <config.yaml> --skip-drc             # Generate without DRC (for iteration)
sonde-hw export <config.yaml>                       # Gerber + BOM + CPL only (assumes .kicad_pcb exists)
sonde-hw budget <config.yaml>                       # Power budget calculator
sonde-hw check-firmware <firmware.bin> [--allow <check-id>]  # Check compiled firmware against hardware
# Commands that perform checks accept repeated --allow <check-id> flags to waive specific findings.
```

HW-1100 states the contract lives at a fixed path (`hw/contract.yaml`), but hw-design.md later describes generating per-config contracts under `hw/output/<config>/contract.yaml`. Please reconcile the location/path expectations (e.g., define `hw/contract-schema.json` as the stable schema file and specify that the contract output is per-config under `hw/output/...`).
```suggestion
1. Each hardware configuration has a generated YAML contract file
   at `hw/output/<config>/contract.yaml`, validated against a stable
   JSON Schema at `hw/contract-schema.json`.
```

HW-0801 acceptance criteria references ΓÇ£KiCad's autorouterΓÇ¥, but KiCad doesn't provide a built-in autorouter in the core toolchain; requiring it makes this criterion ambiguous/unverifiable. Consider rewording to explicitly allow a third-party autorouter plugin, or require scripted routing / deterministic interactive routing steps instead.
```suggestion
3. Trace routing is performed by either (a) a deterministic scripted
   routing algorithm (e.g., a KiCad Action Plugin or external autorouter
   invoked via script) or (b) a documented, replayable set of interactive
   routing steps. Manual routing adjustments are allowed post-generation.
```
